info.title: GNU-TK
info.description: Provides portable access to GNU toolkits.

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Local GNU system.
Location: /
Semantics: GNU
Isolation: none
Check status: ✔ PASS
Tips:
- To list all available toolkits, use 'gnu-tk list' command
- To change the selected toolkit, specify '--toolkit' command-line option or
its shorthand '-t'
- Alternatively, you can set 'GNU_TK_TOOLKIT' environment variable to the
name of a GNU toolkit to use by default

options.name: --help
options.description:
Show this help.
-q --quiet           Do not print any auxiliary messages.
<path>               Path to translate.
-g --guest           Convert path to guest system format.
-h --host            Convert path to host system format.
-a --absolute        Produce absolute path.
-t --toolkit=<name>  Use the specified GNU toolkit [default: auto].
-s --strict          Use a toolkit with strict GNU semantics.
-i --integrated      Use a toolkit that operates within the host environment.

options.name: --version
options.description:
Show version.
-c --command         Execute a command using a GNU shell.
-l --command-line    Execute a command line using a GNU shell.
-f --file            Execute a script using a GNU shell.
-x --execute         Execute a file in a GNU environment.
-q --quiet           Do not print any auxiliary messages.
-t --toolkit=<name>  Use the specified GNU toolkit [default: auto].
-s --strict          Use a toolkit with strict GNU semantics.
-i --integrated      Use a toolkit that operates within the host environment.
-p --posix           Enables POSIX-compliant behavior.

options.name: --verbatim
options.description: Pass command-line arguments as-is, without escaping.
